title: Salty Midnight Canadian Poutine Fries
description: A Canadian dish that will satisfy your midnight cravings with a mix of salty, snappy, and melty goodness. Vegetarian-friendly and perfect for a quick snack.

ingredients.name: Manteca
ingredients.notes: Vegetarian manteca or other emulsified lard substitute

instructions: Cut the fries to your desired thickness and soak them in cold water for at least 30 minutes to remove excess starch.
Heat the manteca in a deep frying pan to 350°F (175°C).
Fry the potatoes in batches until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per batch.
Drain the fries on paper towels and season with salt and pepper to taste.
In a separate saucepan, melt 1/2 cup of Colby Jack cheese over low heat.
Stir in the Worcestershire sauce, if using.
Assemble the poutine by placing the fries in a bowl, topping with cheese sauce, and sprinkling with chopped chives and additional salt to taste.
